articles.title: Trump says 48 leaders killed in strikes on Iran, and new leaders want to talk

articles.title: Crew member killed after projectile hits tanker off Oman, manager says

articles.title: OpenClaw, but in containers: Meet NanoClaw
articles.summary:
A smaller, security-conscious take on the viral AI agent platform

Interview  Ideally, you shouldn't have to defend yourself against your own AI agent. But we don't live in an ideal world and an unrestrained agent can cause a ton of damage.…
articles.reasoning: The article discusses security and control mechanisms for AI agent platforms, focusing on secure design, auditability, and containerization to manage risk, which aligns with IT governance principles of ensuring IT systems are managed securely and effectively.

articles.title: Engineer held hostage by client who asked for the wrong fix
articles.summary:
'I was no longer field support. I was collateral'

On Call  Friday has arrived, bringing a promise of fleeting freedom – and a new instalment of On Call, The Register's reader-contributed column that retells your tales of tech support incidents that became memorable for all the wrong reasons.…
articles.reasoning: The article describes a tech support incident involving a field engineer resolving a hardware failure (failed system board), which aligns with the incident management process of restoring normal service operation as quickly as possible.

articles.title: ServiceNow boasts its AI bot is resolving 90% of its own help desk tickets
articles.summary:
When it gets stuck, the bot will escalate rather than hallucinate

articles.reasoning: The article focuses on an AI agent autonomously resolving IT help desk tickets, which is a core function of incident management, including handling high-volume issues like password resets and account unlocks within defined escalation paths.
